ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the site */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the site */,
  "site_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the site for easy reference */,
  "site_status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the site (e.g., active, maintenance, offline) */,
  "site_manager" TEXT /* Name of the site manager */,
  "site_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the site manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ups_units" (
  "ups_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each UPS unit */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site where the UPS is deployed */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the UPS unit */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the UPS unit */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the UPS unit was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date the UPS unit was last maintained */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the UPS unit for remote monitoring */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Current firmware version of the UPS unit */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Rated capacity of the UPS unit in VA (Volt-Amperes) */,
  "max_load" INTEGER /* Maximum load the UPS can handle in VA */,
  "current_load" INTEGER /* Current load on the UPS in VA */,
  "battery_capacity" INTEGER /* Current battery capacity in percentage */,
  "battery_runtime" INTEGER /* Estimated runtime in minutes at the current load */,
  "temperature" INTEGER /* Internal temperature of the UPS in degrees Celsius */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Internal humidity of the UPS in percentage */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status (e.g., online, offline, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ups_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ups_units_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "light_indicators" (
  "light_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each light indicator */,
  "light_name" TEXT /* Name of the light indicator */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the light indicator */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the light indicator */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the light indicator on the UPS unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("light_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "light_states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "light_id" INTEGER /* ID of the light indicator */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state (e.g., ON, OFF, Blinks) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the state indicates */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the state (e.g., info, warning, critical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_light_states_light_id FOREIGN KEY ("light_id") REFERENCES light_indicators ("light_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ups_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "ups_id" INTEGER /* ID of the UPS unit */,
  "light_id" INTEGER /* ID of the light indicator */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the status update */,
  "input_voltage" INTEGER /* Input voltage in volts */,
  "output_voltage" INTEGER /* Output voltage in volts */,
  "input_frequency" INTEGER /* Input frequency in Hz */,
  "output_frequency" INTEGER /* Output frequency in Hz */,
  "load_percentage" INTEGER /* Load percentage of the UPS */,
  "battery_health" TEXT /* Health status of the battery (e.g., good, degraded, critical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ups_status_ups_id FOREIGN KEY ("ups_id") REFERENCES ups_units ("ups_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ups_status_light_id FOREIGN KEY ("light_id") REFERENCES light_indicators ("light_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ups_status_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES light_states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarms" (
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm */,
  "ups_id" INTEGER /* ID of the UPS unit */,
  "alarm_code" TEXT /* Code of the alarm */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alarm */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the alarm */,
  "acknowledged" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the alarm has been acknowledged */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the alarm (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the alarm has been resolved */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on how the alarm was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alarm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_ups_id FOREIGN KEY ("ups_id") REFERENCES ups_units ("ups_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "ups_id" INTEGER /* ID of the UPS unit */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance activity */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., routine, emergency, software update) */,
  "maintenance_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the maintenance in minutes */,
  "maintenance_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the maintenance activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_ups_id FOREIGN KEY ("ups_id") REFERENCES ups_units ("ups_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES technicians ("technician_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "technicians" (
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each technician */,
  "technician_name" TEXT /* Full name of the technician */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the technician */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the technician */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the technician belongs to */,
  "certifications" TEXT /* Certifications held by the technician */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the technician is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("technician_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, technician, support) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client used to access the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message of the log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the log entry */,
  "ups_id" INTEGER /* ID of the UPS unit associated with the log entry */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the log entry (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log entry (e.g., user, system, UPS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_logs_ups_id FOREIGN KEY ("ups_id") REFERENCES ups_units ("ups_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_logs" (
  "alert_log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert log entry */,
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alarm */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the alert log entry */,
  "acknowledged" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the alert has been acknowledged */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_logs_alarm_id FOREIGN KEY ("alarm_id") REFERENCES alarms ("alarm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_logs_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration entry */,
  "ups_id" INTEGER /* ID of the UPS unit */,
  "config_key" TEXT /* Key of the configuration setting */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  "config_type" TEXT /* Type of configuration (e.g., system, UPS) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_ups_id FOREIGN KEY ("ups_id") REFERENCES ups_units ("ups_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "documentation" (
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "ups_id" INTEGER /* ID of the UPS unit */,
  "doc_type" TEXT /* Type of the document (e.g., manual, guide) */,
  "doc_title" TEXT /* Title of the document */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the document */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the document */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the document */,
  "approved_by" TEXT /* Name of the person who approved the document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doc_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_documentation_ups_id FOREIGN KEY ("ups_id") REFERENCES ups_units ("ups_id")
)
